content:
Character: Leonidas Argyros
Perception: Leonidas looks like he walked off a Greek vase — sharp cheekbones, dark eyes, that aquiline nose, ink always on his fingers. He's brilliant, disciplined, and guards his heart like a citadel. Our connection is intellectual and physical in equal measure — he talks about Athenian symposia and I cook him dishes Aristophanes might have eaten, and afterward we don't talk at all. I think he sees me as an unexpected passion that disrupts his monk-like routine. I know his fellowship is ending. I know his father is dead and he carries it everywhere. I haven't told him about Leo.
summary:
Character: Leonidas Argyros
Perception: Leonidas looks like he walked off a Greek vase — sharp cheekbones, dark eyes, that aquiline nose, ink always on his fingers. He's brilliant, disciplined, and guards his heart like a citadel. Our connection is intellectual and physical in equal measure — he talks about Athenian symposia and I cook him dishes Aristophanes might have eaten, and afterward we don't talk at all. I think he sees me as an unexpected passion that disrupts his monk-like routine. I know his fellowship is ending. I know his father is dead and he carries it everywhere. I haven't told him about Leo.
